docs.title: zig-ctap2
docs.text:
Portable CTAP2/FIDO2 library in Zig -- direct USB HID communication with security keys (YubiKey, SoloKeys, etc.), no Apple entitlements or platform authentication frameworks needed.

License: Zlib OR MIT

docs.title: Why
docs.text: Apple's ASAuthorizationController requires a restricted entitlement + provisioning profile for WebAuthn in general-purpose browsers. This library talks directly to FIDO2 devices over USB HID via IOKit (macOS) and hidraw (Linux), bypassing platform authentication frameworks entirely.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• CTAP2 protocol: makeCredential, getAssertion, getInfo, with structured response parsing
  • PIN protocol v2: ECDH P-256 key agreement, AES-256-CBC, HMAC-SHA-256 for PIN-authenticated operations
  • CTAPHID framing: 64-byte packet fragmentation/reassembly, CID management, keepalive handling
  • Minimal CBOR codec: encoder/decoder for the CTAP2 subset (integers, byte/text strings, arrays, maps, booleans)
  • Platform HID transports: macOS (IOKit), Linux (hidraw)
  • C FFI: 16+ exported functions callable from Swift, C, C++, or any language with C interop
  • Error mapping: All CTAP2 status codes mapped to human-readable messages
  • Property-based tests: 1000-iteration roundtrip tests for CBOR and CTAPHID framing

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
# Build static library (libctap2.a)
zig build -Doptimize=ReleaseFast

# Run unit tests
zig build test

# Run property-based tests
zig build test-pbt

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
graph TD
    A[Application / Browser] -->|C FFI| B[ffi.zig]
    B --> C[ctap2.zig<br/>Commands + Response Parsing]
    B --> D[pin.zig<br/>PIN Protocol v2]
    C --> E[cbor.zig<br/>CBOR Codec]
    D --> E
    C --> F[ctaphid.zig<br/>HID Framing]
    D --> F
    F --> G{Platform}
    G -->|macOS| H[hid_macos.zig<br/>IOKit HID]
    G -->|Linux| I[hid_linux.zig<br/>hidraw]

docs.title: Registration Flow
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant App
    participant FFI as ffi.zig
    participant CTAP as ctap2.zig
    participant CBOR as cbor.zig
    participant HID as ctaphid.zig
    participant Key as YubiKey

    App->>FFI: ctap2_make_credential_parsed()
    FFI->>CTAP: encodeMakeCredential()
    CTAP->>CBOR: CBOR encode request
    CBOR-->>CTAP: bytes
    CTAP->>HID: CTAPHID_CBOR (fragmented)
    HID->>Key: USB HID packets
    Note over Key: User touches key
    Key-->>HID: Response packets
    HID-->>CTAP: Reassembled CBOR
    CTAP->>CTAP: parseMakeCredentialResponse()
    CTAP-->>FFI: credential_id, attestation_object
    FFI-->>App: Structured result

docs.title: Source Tree
docs.text:
zig-ctap2/
  build.zig          -- Build configuration
  include/
    ctap2.h          -- C header (public API)
  src/
    ffi.zig          -- C FFI exports
    ctap2.zig        -- CTAP2 command encoding + response parsing
    cbor.zig         -- Minimal CBOR codec
    ctaphid.zig      -- CTAPHID framing (64-byte packets)
    hid.zig          -- Platform HID abstraction
    hid_macos.zig    -- IOKit HID transport
    hid_linux.zig    -- hidraw transport
    pin.zig          -- PIN protocol v2
  tests/
    pbt_cbor.zig     -- CBOR property-based tests
    pbt_ctaphid.zig  -- CTAPHID property-based tests

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Zig 0.15.2+
  • macOS 13+ (IOKit) or Linux (hidraw)
  • USB security key (tested with YubiKey 5C NFC)

docs.title: Status
docs.text:
• makeCredential (registration)
  • getAssertion (authentication)
  • getInfo (device capabilities)
  • CBOR response parsing (structured result types)
  • CTAP2 error code mapping (human-readable messages)
  • PIN protocol v2 (ECDH P-256, AES-256-CBC, HMAC-SHA-256)
  • Property-based tests (CBOR + CTAPHID, 1000 iterations each)
  • Hardware integration tests (YubiKey roundtrips)
  • Extensions (credProtect, hmac-secret)
  • NFC transport

docs.title: AGENTS.md
docs.text: Instructions for AI agents working with this codebase.

docs.title: Project
docs.text: zig-ctap2 is a portable CTAP2/FIDO2 library written in Zig. It communicates directly with USB HID security keys via IOKit (macOS) and hidraw (Linux), exposing a C FFI for integration into Swift, C, and other languages.

docs.title: Build
docs.text:
zig build -Doptimize=ReleaseFast    # static library
zig build test                       # unit tests
zig build test-pbt                   # property-based tests

docs.title: Structure
docs.text:
• include/ctap2.h -- Public C API header
  • src/ffi.zig -- C FFI export layer
  • src/ctap2.zig -- CTAP2 command encoding and response parsing
  • src/cbor.zig -- CBOR codec (CTAP2 subset)
  • src/ctaphid.zig -- CTAPHID packet framing
  • src/hid.zig -- Platform HID abstraction
  • src/pin.zig -- PIN protocol v2 (ECDH, AES-256-CBC)
  • tests/ -- Property-based tests

docs.title: Conventions
docs.text:
• All exported C functions use snake_case with ctap2_ prefix
  • Zig internal functions use camelCase
  • All C functions are blocking with timeouts and thread-safe
  • Output data is written to caller-provided buffers
  • Error codes: 0 = success, negative = library error, positive = CTAP2 device status byte
  • Platform-specific code lives in _macos.zig / _linux.zig suffixed files

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
• Unit tests: zig build test (no hardware required)
  • Property-based tests: zig build test-pbt (1000 iterations)
  • Hardware tests: YUBIKEY_TESTS=1 zig build test-hardware (YubiKey required)

docs.title: ctap2.h
docs.text: Function Description ctap2_device_count Get the number of connected FIDO2 devices. ctap2_make_credential Perform authenticatorMakeCredential. client_data_hash must be 32 bytes (SHA-256 of clientDataJSON). Returns bytes written to result_buf, or negative error code. result_buf contains the raw CTAP2 response (status byte + CBOR). ctap2_get_assertion Perform authenticatorGetAssertion. client_data_hash must be 32 bytes. allow_list_ids is an array of pointers to credential IDs. allow_list_id_lens is an array of lengths for each credential ID. Returns bytes written to result_buf, or negative error code. ctap2_get_info Perform authenticatorGetInfo. Returns bytes written to result_buf, or negative error code. result_buf contains the raw CTAP2 response (status byte + CBOR). ctap2_make_credential_parsed Combined: send makeCredential + parse response. Output buffers should be at least 1024 bytes for credential_id, and 4096 bytes for attestation_object. ctap2_get_assertion_parsed Combined: send getAssertion + parse response. Output buffers should be at least 1024 bytes each. allow_list_ids/allow_list_id_lens can be NULL when allow_list_count is 0. ctap2_parse_make_credential_response Parse a raw MakeCredential response (status byte + CBOR attestation object). ctap2_parse_get_assertion_response Parse a raw GetAssertion response (status byte + CBOR). fallback_cred_id: credential ID to use when the response omits key 1 (CTAP2 spec: single-entry allowList). Pass NULL/0 if no fallback. ctap2_get_pin_retries Get PIN retry count from the authenticator. out_retries: receives the number of remaining PIN retries. Returns CTAP2_OK on success, or negative error code. ctap2_get_pin_token Get a PIN token for authentication. Performs the full PIN protocol v2 handshake (key agreement + ECDH + PIN encryption) and returns a decrypted 32-byte PIN token. pin: null-terminated UTF-8 PIN string. out_pin_token: receives the 32-byte decrypted PIN token. out_pin_token_len: must be >= 32. Returns CTAP2_OK on success, positive CTAP2 status byte on device error (e.g. 0x31 = wrong PIN), or negative error code. ctap2_make_credential_with_pin Same as the parsed functions above, but with optional PIN auth. Pass pin_token=NULL, pin_protocol=0 for no PIN authentication. Pass pin_token=<32-byte token from ctap2_get_pin_token>, pin_protocol=2 to include pinAuth in the CTAP2 command. ctap2_get_assertion_with_pin ctap2_get_assertion_with_pin ctap2_make_credential_with_keepalive ctap2_make_credential_with_keepalive ctap2_get_assertion_with_keepalive ctap2_get_assertion_with_keepalive ctap2_debug_last_ioreturn Debug: get the last IOReturn error code from HID operations.

docs.title: ctap2_device_count
docs.text:
Get the number of connected FIDO2 devices.

int ctap2_device_count(void);

docs.title: ctap2_make_credential
docs.text:
Perform authenticatorMakeCredential. client_data_hash must be 32 bytes (SHA-256 of clientDataJSON). Returns bytes written to result_buf, or negative error code. result_buf contains the raw CTAP2 response (status byte + CBOR).

int ctap2_make_credential( const uint8_t *client_data_hash, // 32 bytes const char *rp_id, // null-terminated const char *rp_name, // null-terminated const uint8_t *user_id, size_t user_id_len, const char *user_name, // null-terminated const char *user_display_name, // null-terminated const int32_t *alg_ids, // COSE algorithm IDs size_t alg_count, bool resident_key, uint8_t *result_buf, size_t result_buf_len );

docs.title: ctap2_get_assertion
docs.text:
Perform authenticatorGetAssertion. client_data_hash must be 32 bytes. allow_list_ids is an array of pointers to credential IDs. allow_list_id_lens is an array of lengths for each credential ID. Returns bytes written to result_buf, or negative error code.

int ctap2_get_assertion( const uint8_t *client_data_hash, // 32 bytes const char *rp_id, // null-terminated const uint8_t *const *allow_list_ids, const size_t *allow_list_id_lens, size_t allow_list_count, uint8_t *result_buf, size_t result_buf_len );

docs.title: ctap2_get_info
docs.text:
Perform authenticatorGetInfo. Returns bytes written to result_buf, or negative error code. result_buf contains the raw CTAP2 response (status byte + CBOR).

int ctap2_get_info( uint8_t *result_buf, size_t result_buf_len );

docs.title: ctap2_make_credential_parsed
docs.text:
Combined: send makeCredential + parse response. Output buffers should be at least 1024 bytes for credential_id, and 4096 bytes for attestation_object.

int ctap2_make_credential_parsed( const uint8_t *client_data_hash, // 32 bytes const char *rp_id, // null-terminated const char *rp_name, // null-terminated const uint8_t *user_id, size_t user_id_len, const char *user_name, // null-terminated const char *user_display_name, // null-terminated const int32_t *alg_ids, // COSE algorithm IDs size_t alg_count, bool resident_key, // Output fields: uint8_t *out_credential_id, size_t *out_credential_id_len, uint8_t *out_attestation_object, size_t *out_attestation_object_len );

docs.title: ctap2_get_assertion_parsed
docs.text:
Combined: send getAssertion + parse response. Output buffers should be at least 1024 bytes each. allow_list_ids/allow_list_id_lens can be NULL when allow_list_count is 0.

int ctap2_get_assertion_parsed( const uint8_t *client_data_hash, // 32 bytes const char *rp_id, // null-terminated const uint8_t *const *allow_list_ids, // nullable const size_t *allow_list_id_lens, // nullable size_t allow_list_count, // Output fields: uint8_t *out_credential_id, size_t *out_credential_id_len, uint8_t *out_auth_data, size_t *out_auth_data_len, uint8_t *out_signature, size_t *out_signature_len, uint8_t *out_user_handle, size_t *out_user_handle_len );

docs.title: ctap2_parse_make_credential_response
docs.text:
Parse a raw MakeCredential response (status byte + CBOR attestation object).

int ctap2_parse_make_credential_response( const uint8_t *response_data, size_t response_len, uint8_t *out_credential_id, size_t *out_credential_id_len, uint8_t *out_attestation_object, size_t *out_attestation_object_len );

docs.title: ctap2_parse_get_assertion_response
docs.text:
Parse a raw GetAssertion response (status byte + CBOR). fallback_cred_id: credential ID to use when the response omits key 1 (CTAP2 spec: single-entry allowList). Pass NULL/0 if no fallback.

int ctap2_parse_get_assertion_response( const uint8_t *response_data, size_t response_len, const uint8_t *fallback_cred_id, // nullable size_t fallback_cred_id_len, uint8_t *out_credential_id, size_t *out_credential_id_len, uint8_t *out_auth_data, size_t *out_auth_data_len, uint8_t *out_signature, size_t *out_signature_len, uint8_t *out_user_handle, size_t *out_user_handle_len );

docs.title: ctap2_get_pin_retries
docs.text:
Get PIN retry count from the authenticator. out_retries: receives the number of remaining PIN retries. Returns CTAP2_OK on success, or negative error code.

int ctap2_get_pin_retries(int *out_retries);

docs.title: ctap2_get_pin_token
docs.text:
Get a PIN token for authentication. Performs the full PIN protocol v2 handshake (key agreement + ECDH + PIN encryption) and returns a decrypted 32-byte PIN token. pin: null-terminated UTF-8 PIN string. out_pin_token: receives the 32-byte decrypted PIN token. out_pin_token_len: must be >= 32. Returns CTAP2_OK on success, positive CTAP2 status byte on device error (e.g. 0x31 = wrong PIN), or negative error code.

int ctap2_get_pin_token( const char *pin, uint8_t *out_pin_token, size_t out_pin_token_len );

docs.title: ctap2_make_credential_with_pin
docs.text:
Same as the parsed functions above, but with optional PIN auth. Pass pin_token=NULL, pin_protocol=0 for no PIN authentication. Pass pin_token=<32-byte token from ctap2_get_pin_token>, pin_protocol=2 to include pinAuth in the CTAP2 command.

int ctap2_make_credential_with_pin( const uint8_t *client_data_hash, // 32 bytes const char *rp_id, // null-terminated const char *rp_name, // null-terminated const uint8_t *user_id, size_t user_id_len, const char *user_name, // null-terminated const char *user_display_name, // null-terminated const int32_t *alg_ids, // COSE algorithm IDs size_t alg_count, bool resident_key, const uint8_t *pin_token, // 32 bytes, or NULL for no PIN uint8_t pin_protocol, // 0 = no PIN, 2 = PIN protocol v2 // Output fields: uint8_t *out_credential_id, size_t *out_credential_id_len, uint8_t *out_attestation_object, size_t *out_attestation_object_len );

docs.title: ctap2_get_assertion_with_pin
docs.text: int ctap2_get_assertion_with_pin( const uint8_t *client_data_hash, // 32 bytes const char *rp_id, // null-terminated const uint8_t *const *allow_list_ids, // nullable const size_t *allow_list_id_lens, // nullable size_t allow_list_count, const uint8_t *pin_token, // 32 bytes, or NULL for no PIN uint8_t pin_protocol, // 0 = no PIN, 2 = PIN protocol v2 // Output fields: uint8_t *out_credential_id, size_t *out_credential_id_len, uint8_t *out_auth_data, size_t *out_auth_data_len, uint8_t *out_signature, size_t *out_signature_len, uint8_t *out_user_handle, size_t *out_user_handle_len );

docs.title: ctap2_make_credential_with_keepalive
docs.text: int ctap2_make_credential_with_keepalive( const uint8_t *client_data_hash, const char *rp_id, const char *rp_name, const uint8_t *user_id, size_t user_id_len, const char *user_name, const char *user_display_name, const int32_t *alg_ids, size_t alg_count, bool resident_key, ctap2_keepalive_callback_t keepalive_cb, uint8_t *result_buf, size_t result_buf_len );

docs.title: parseMakeCredentialResponse
docs.text:
Parse a raw CTAP2 authenticatorMakeCredential response. The response format is: status_byte(1) + CBOR_map The CBOR map has integer keys: 1 = fmt (text string) 2 = authData (byte string) 3 = attStmt (map) From authData we extract the credential ID: rpIdHash(32) + flags(1) + signCount(4) + [aaguid(16) + credIdLen(2) + credentialId(credIdLen) + ...]

pub fn parseMakeCredentialResponse(response_data: []const u8) cbor.Error!MakeCredentialResult

docs.title: parseGetAssertionResponse
docs.text:
Parse a raw CTAP2 authenticatorGetAssertion response. The response format is: status_byte(1) + CBOR_map The CBOR map has integer keys: 1 = credential (map with "id" byte string) — optional per spec 2 = authData (byte string) 3 = signature (byte string) 4 = user (map with "id" byte string) — optional Per CTAP2 spec: key 1 (credential) is omitted when the allowList in the request had exactly one entry. In that case, use the fallback credential ID.

pub fn parseGetAssertionResponse( response_data: []const u8, fallback_cred_id: ?[]const u8, ) cbor.Error!GetAssertionResult

docs.title: ffi.zig

docs.title: CoseKey (struct)
docs.text: A COSE_Key for EC2 P-256 (used in key agreement).

docs.title: EphemeralKeyPair (struct)
docs.text: Ephemeral key pair for ECDH key agreement.

docs.title: SharedSecret (struct)
docs.text: Shared secret derived from ECDH.

docs.title: PINTokenResult (struct)
docs.text: Result from getPINToken.

docs.title: generateKeyPair
docs.text:
Generate an ephemeral ECDH P-256 key pair for key agreement.

pub fn generateKeyPair() EphemeralKeyPair

docs.title: deriveSharedSecret
docs.text:
Perform ECDH: multiply their public point by our private scalar. Returns SHA-256 of the x-coordinate of the shared point.

pub fn deriveSharedSecret( our_private: [32]u8, their_public: CoseKey, ) !SharedSecret

docs.title: computeHmac
docs.text:
Compute HMAC-SHA-256(key, message).

pub fn computeHmac(key: []const u8, message: []const u8) [32]u8

docs.title: computePinAuth
docs.text:
Compute pinAuth: first 16 bytes of HMAC-SHA-256(pinToken, message). Used for authenticating commands with a PIN token.

pub fn computePinAuth(pin_token: []const u8, message: []const u8) [16]u8

docs.title: aes256CbcEncrypt
docs.text:
AES-256-CBC encrypt (with zero IV, per CTAP2 PIN protocol v2 spec). Input must be a multiple of 16 bytes. Returns the ciphertext (same length as input).

pub fn aes256CbcEncrypt( key: [32]u8, plaintext: []const u8, out: []u8, ) !void

docs.title: aes256CbcDecrypt
docs.text:
AES-256-CBC decrypt (with zero IV, per CTAP2 PIN protocol v2 spec). Input must be a multiple of 16 bytes. Returns the plaintext (same length as input).

pub fn aes256CbcDecrypt( key: [32]u8, ciphertext: []const u8, out: []u8, ) !void

docs.title: encodeGetPINRetries
docs.text:
Encode a getPINRetries request. Request: {1: pinUvAuthProtocol(2), 2: subCommand(1)}

pub fn encodeGetPINRetries(buf: []u8) cbor.Error![]const u8

docs.title: encodeGetKeyAgreement
docs.text:
Encode a getKeyAgreement request. Request: {1: pinUvAuthProtocol(2), 2: subCommand(2)}

pub fn encodeGetKeyAgreement(buf: []u8) cbor.Error![]const u8

docs.title: encodeGetPINToken
docs.text:
Encode a getPinUvAuthTokenUsingPinWithPermissions request (subCommand 0x09). Request: {1: protocol, 2: subCommand(9), 3: keyAgreement(COSE_Key), 6: pinHashEnc}

pub fn encodeGetPINToken( buf: []u8, our_public_key: CoseKey, pin_hash_enc: []const u8, ) cbor.Error![]const u8

docs.title: parsePINRetriesResponse
docs.text:
Parse a getPINRetries response. Response CBOR (after status byte): {3: pinRetries, 4: powerCycleState(optional)}

pub fn parsePINRetriesResponse(response_data: []const u8) !PINRetriesResult

docs.title: parseKeyAgreementResponse
docs.text:
Parse a getKeyAgreement response. Response CBOR (after status byte): {1: keyAgreement(COSE_Key)} COSE_Key: {1: kty(2), 3: alg(-25), -1: crv(1), -2: x(32 bytes), -3: y(32 bytes)}

pub fn parseKeyAgreementResponse(response_data: []const u8) !CoseKey

docs.title: parsePINTokenResponse
docs.text:
Parse a getPINToken response. Response CBOR (after status byte): {2: pinUvAuthToken(encrypted bytes)}

pub fn parsePINTokenResponse( response_data: []const u8, shared_secret: SharedSecret, ) !PINTokenResult

docs.title: encryptPINHash
docs.text:
Prepare the encrypted PIN hash for a getPINToken request. Takes a UTF-8 PIN string, hashes it with SHA-256, takes the first 16 bytes, pads to 64 bytes, and encrypts with AES-256-CBC using the shared secret. Returns the 64-byte encrypted PIN hash.

pub fn encryptPINHash( pin: []const u8, shared_secret: SharedSecret, ) ![64]u8

docs.title: encodeMakeCredentialWithPIN
docs.text:
Encode a makeCredential command with pinAuth and pinUvAuthProtocol. This adds parameters 8 (pinUvAuthProtocol) and 9 (pinAuth) to the command. pinAuth = LEFT(HMAC-SHA-256(pinToken, clientDataHash), 16)

pub fn encodeMakeCredentialWithPIN( buf: []u8, client_data_hash: []const u8, rp_id: []const u8, rp_name: []const u8, user_id: []const u8, user_name: []const u8, user_display_name: []const u8, algorithms: []const i32, resident_key: bool, pin_token: [32]u8, ) cbor.Error![]const u8

docs.title: encodeGetAssertionWithPIN
docs.text:
Encode a getAssertion command with pinAuth and pinUvAuthProtocol.

pub fn encodeGetAssertionWithPIN( buf: []u8, rp_id: []const u8, client_data_hash: []const u8, allow_list_ids: []const []const u8, pin_token: [32]u8, ) cbor.Error![]const u8

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Zig 0.15.2+
  • macOS 13+ or Linux

docs.title: Static Library
docs.text:
zig build -Doptimize=ReleaseFast

This produces zig-out/lib/libctap2.a with the C header at include/ctap2.h.

docs.title: With just
docs.text:
just build         # ReleaseFast static library
just test-all      # unit + PBT tests
just info          # show library stats
just               # list all recipes

docs.title: With Nix
docs.text:
nix develop        # dev shell (zig, just, detect-secrets, pre-commit)
nix build          # build library package

docs.title: Running Tests
docs.text:
# Unit tests (no hardware required)
zig build test

# Property-based tests (1000 iterations each)
zig build test-pbt

# Hardware tests (requires YubiKey connected)
YUBIKEY_TESTS=1 zig build test-hardware

docs.title: Cross-Compilation
docs.text:
The library supports cross-compilation to any Zig target. IOKit and CoreFoundation frameworks are not linked at build time -- they resolve at final link time in the consuming application (e.g., via Xcode OTHER_LDFLAGS).

# Build for specific target
zig build -Dtarget=aarch64-macos
zig build -Dtarget=x86_64-linux

docs.title: macOS Entitlements
docs.text:
When embedding in a hardened-runtime macOS app, add to your entitlements:

<key>com.apple.security.device.usb</key>
<true/>

The user must grant Input Monitoring permission in System Settings > Privacy & Security.

No other entitlements needed -- no com.apple.developer.web-browser.public-key-credential, no provisioning profile.

docs.title: As a Zig Dependency
docs.text:
Add zig-ctap2 as a dependency in your build.zig.zon:

.dependencies = .{
    .ctap2 = .{
        .url = "https://github.com/Jesssullivan/zig-ctap2/archive/refs/heads/main.tar.gz",
    },
},

Then in build.zig:

const ctap2_dep = b.dependency("ctap2", .{
    .target = target,
    .optimize = optimize,
});
exe.linkLibrary(ctap2_dep.artifact("ctap2"));

docs.title: As a C Static Library
docs.text:
Build the library:

zig build -Doptimize=ReleaseFast

Link against zig-out/lib/libctap2.a and include include/ctap2.h:

#include "ctap2.h"

int main() {
    int count = ctap2_device_count();
    printf("Found %d FIDO2 devices\n", count);
    return 0;
}

Compile:

gcc -o example example.c -Iinclude -Lzig-out/lib -lctap2

docs.title: Swift Integration (Xcode)
docs.text:
1. Add zig-ctap2 as a git submodule or copy libctap2.a + ctap2.h into your project
  2. Add ctap2.h to your bridging header
  3. Link libctap2.a in your target's build settings
  4. Add IOKit.framework and CoreFoundation.framework to linked frameworks
  5. Add USB entitlement to your .entitlements file
import Foundation

let deviceCount = ctap2_device_count()
print("Found \(deviceCount) FIDO2 devices")

docs.title: Git Submodule
docs.text: git submodule add https://github.com/Jesssullivan/zig-ctap2.git vendor/ctap2

docs.title: Integration with cmux
docs.text: This library powers the FIDO2/WebAuthn support in cmux, integrated as a git submodule at vendor/ctap2. The JS bridge in WKWebView intercepts navigator.credentials.create/get and routes to libctap2 via Swift C FFI.
